channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17231] Create man(4) pages for tunable parameters
item.description:
Lustre has a number of tunable parameters, but they are not very well documented.

It would be useful to start adding man pages in section 4 ("kernel interfaces/special files") for each of the tunable parameters and stats files. This will obviously need a bunch of separate patches, but needs to start somewhere.
item.summary: Create man(4) pages for tunable parameters

status.description: This issue was once resolved, but the resolution was deemed incorrect. From here issues are either marked assigned or resolved.

comments.comment: Hmm, it looks like patch https://review.whamcloud.com/61748 ("LU-17231 doc: Added man pages for adaptive timeouts") is answering this question already. It contains separate man pages for each of different places where at_min, at_max, and at_history may appear. It looks like this just needs to be documented clearly here and in the Git tree.

fdilger, one question that needs to be answered here is whether parameters that are specific to device types (e.g. mdt.*.enable_remote_dir) should include the device name into the man page path (e.g. mdt.enable_remote_dir), and parameters that are shared between device types (e.g. osc.*.max_rpcs_in_flight and mdc.*.max_rpcs_in_flight) should pick one of those names (e.g. osc.max_rpcs_in_flight.4) to hold the content and then reference that page from the other device names (e.g. mdc.max_rpcs_in_flight.4 contains ".so osc.max_rpcs_in_flight.4"), or should the filenames just be the "base" name (e.g. max_rpcs_in_flight)?

Most of the parameters that have been documented so far are "global" parameters that are located at the top-level of the parameter hierarchy and not instantiated on a per-device basis. Having the per-device parameters use a single "global" name seems OK at first glance and reduces some overhead, but I worry about device-specific parameters that might already have the same name, but actually have different contents/meanings. It is easy to pick the type-specific filenames today, even if there may be several instances of these files (e.g. kbytes or state or pool), but it would be hard to disambiguate them in the future if there are conflicts.

It would be great to edit the initial description in this ticket to explain the attributes of an ideal parameter man page, so that it can be used for reference in the future. Possibly also in a Documentation/man4/README file for reference in the Git repo.

"Ellis Wilson <elliswilson@microsoft.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62950
Subject: LU-17231 doc: Add man pages for Layout & Stripe Parameters
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 907bf5276f47078dd15d46c7fd5335f72566e446

As it happens I am done revisions to all of the other pages in https://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LUDOC-542 and am moving onto the Lustre Tuning page for our next meeting.  I realize here you are referring to documenting them in man pages, but I'm beginning with the wiki pages in the DWG due to the relative age of those pages and the relatively higher hit-rate for them (compared to man pages) when people are searching online for something like Lustre Tuning.  I have a slew of historical Lustre Tuning pages and parameters bookmarked, but if you wanted to put a short (or long) list of them here I would be glad to research them if I don't already know what they do, and include them.  My hope is to keep Lustre Tuning from becoming completionist – Lustre already gets a bad rap about the deep need to tune it, which is unfair and untrue in many cases, so if you can keep the list to the most important that would be most useful.

elliswilson, I'm not sure if you are aware of this, but it would definitely be something that could benefit from contributions from the LDP. There are lots of Lustre tunables that should be documented.

"Frederick Dilger <fdilger@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61748
Subject: LU-17231 doc: Added man pages for at_* parameters
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 457b2b5d15e68a0bae688111b660b6d3c9d8b197

"Frederick Dilger <fdilger@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61318
Subject: LU-17231 doc: updated man page script
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 09c6eb5ef81be914d98991e00a84a41673a2af28

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60047/
Subject: LU-17231 doc: Create script to generate man pages
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 1792d49a9660271c1a1d60a467096143e8b43816

"Frederick Dilger <fdilger@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60047
Subject: LU-17231 doc: Create script to generate man pages
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: d40c1314283698abe6ade86e093e8df06d9fe342

There should be a consistent naming format used for the man pages related to the parameter name, so that the man page names could be generated automatically by e.g. "lfs get_param". One proposal (possibly a bit lengthy) would be "lctl-param-TARGET.-.NAME" as a general template, and specifically something like "lctl-param-osc.-.max_dirty_mb.4" or similar. I don't think it is possible or practical to embed "*" into the filename, so "-" is one option that could be used to replace it.

Each file would have the parameter name, a one-line description, sample output, and provide a description of the output. This should include if the parameter is global or per-device, and whether it is only present with certain configurations. If the parameter is readable and/or writeable, and whether writing to the parameter will reset it.
